idea.problem: Difficulty finding qualified engineers for media and educational companies despite posting on job boards.
idea.target_user: HR manager at a media or educational company
idea.solution: AI-powered job matching for engineering positions at unique companies like PBS, connecting them with a curated pool of suitable candidates.
idea.why_now: Automation in hiring processes is gaining traction, and the pandemic has led to a shift towards remote work, making this solution more desirable.
idea.existing_spend_behavior: Hiring managers often invest in premium job boards, career website development, and costly recruiters to find the right talent.

idea.problem: Lack of access to a skilled and diverse pool of candidates for engineering positions.
idea.target_user: Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) officer or HR manager at a media company
idea.solution: A platform using AI to identify and connect underrepresented groups in tech with engineering job openings at companies like PBS, promoting diversity and inclusion in the hiring process.
idea.why_now: There is an increasing focus on DEI in the tech industry, and companies are looking for solutions to address these issues.
idea.existing_spend_behavior: DEI officers often invest in diversity training programs, unconscious bias workshops, and affinity groups to promote inclusion in the hiring process.

idea.problem: Large teams struggle to track meeting notes, decisions, and follow-ups in a centralized hub
idea.target_user: Engineer lead at a mid-sized tech company using Notion for collaboration
idea.solution: MeetEcho is a meeting notes management system that integrates with Notion to keep teams on the same page
idea.why_now: Notion's popularity and large team features make it the perfect time to build a solution that fills its gaps
idea.existing_spend_behavior: Engineering teams already spend money on tools like Loom and Slack to manage meetings and communication

signal.source_text: Notion | Engineer, Designer, Sales, Marketing, PM, Support | SF |  https://www.notion.so  | Full-time Onsite == Intro ==================================== Hey there, Notion founder here. At Notion, our goal to create the general purpose work tool for a post-file, post-MS Office world. My cofounder and I often joke that this was like the "hot startup topic" of the 90s. Unfortunately, tools for knowledge workers haven't advanced much since then. The state of art is either Google Docs/Quip/Dropbox Paper (multiplayer WordPerfect), or rigid SAAS apps (forms + a table+ some buttons). This is our product:  https://notion.so  We are looking for people with the following roles:  https://www.notion.so/jobs  We made a graphic novel about why we exist:  https://notion.so/about  Read more reviews on WSJ and Product Hunt. (Notion was one of the fastest voted of all time):  https://www.wsj.com/articles/the-only-app-you-need-for-work-... .  https://www.producthunt.com/posts/notion-2-0  We are a bunch of artsy nerds in SF. The business is profitable and growing very fast. We have a beautiful artist loft in the SF Mission district, with some of the best investors out there (notion.so/about). == Apply ======================================= Please see details here ->>  https://www.notion.so/jobs

NotionFit is a product adoption platform that provides training, onboarding, and analytics to help teams get the most ou

💸 Monthly SaaS subscription — starting at $49/mo

idea.problem: Companies using Notion struggle to ensure that team members are using the tool consistently and effectively
idea.target_user: Product manager at a company using Notion for project management
idea.solution: NotionFit is a product adoption platform that provides training, onboarding, and analytics to help teams get the most out of Notion
idea.why_now: Notion's growth and adoption make it the perfect time to build a solution that helps teams maximize its potential
idea.existing_spend_behavior: Companies already spend money on product adoption platforms like Groove and 15Five to ensure team onboarding and training

signal.source_text: Notion | Engineer, Designer, Sales, Marketing, PM, Support | SF |  https://www.notion.so  | Full-time Onsite == Intro ==================================== Hey there, Notion founder here. At Notion, our goal to create the general purpose work tool for a post-file, post-MS Office world. My cofounder and I often joke that this was like the "hot startup topic" of the 90s. Unfortunately, tools for knowledge workers haven't advanced much since then. The state of art is either Google Docs/Quip/Dropbox Paper (multiplayer WordPerfect), or rigid SAAS apps (forms + a table+ some buttons). This is our product:  https://notion.so  We are looking for people with the following roles:  https://www.notion.so/jobs  We made a graphic novel about why we exist:  https://notion.so/about  Read more reviews on WSJ and Product Hunt. (Notion was one of the fastest voted of all time):  https://www.wsj.com/articles/the-only-app-you-need-for-work-... .  https://www.producthunt.com/posts/notion-2-0  We are a bunch of artsy nerds in SF. The business is profitable and growing very fast. We have a beautiful artist loft in the SF Mission district, with some of the best investors out there (notion.so/about). == Apply ======================================= Please see details here ->>  https://www.notion.so/jobs

idea.problem: Companies face difficulties in posting accurate, consistent job descriptions for senior engineering roles across multiple locations and job boards
idea.target_user: HR Manager at mid-sized to large engineering firms with multiple locations
idea.solution: JobDescriptionAI creates AI-generated, location-specific job descriptions for senior engineering roles, allowing users to easily publish on multiple job boards
idea.why_now: With the rise of remote work and talent scarcity, companies need to tailor their job postings to attract the best candidates, making now the right time to launch this solution
idea.existing_spend_behavior: HR managers already pay for job boards like Indeed, LinkedIn, and Glassdoor to post their job openings, often duplicating efforts across multiple platforms

idea.problem: Streamline onboarding process of Sales talent in remote companies
idea.target_user: HR Manager in a remote company with a team of Sales Development Representatives
idea.solution: An automated, customizable onboarding platform to introduce new Sales hires to company policies, team members, and tools, ensuring seamless integration into the remote sales team
idea.why_now: Remote HR teams face challenges in onboarding and managing a distributed sales team, existing platforms often being rigid and not tailored to sales-specific needs
idea.existing_spend_behavior: Remote companies already spend $500/month on onboarding platforms and $1,000/month on additional training sessions

tagline: An automated, customizable onboarding platform to introduce new Sales hires to company policies, team members, and tools
validation_steps: Post in 3 niche communities where HR Manager in a remote company with a team of Sales Development Representatives hangs out

template:
# API Wrapper MVP starter (FastAPI)
# pip install fastapi uvicorn httpx

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel
import httpx

app = FastAPI(title="An Automated, CustomizableAI", description="An automated, customizable onboarding platform to introduce new Sales hires to company policies, team members, and tools")

class Request(BaseModel):
    input: str

@app.post("/run")
async def run(req: Request):
    """Core endpoint — replace with actual upstream API call."""
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        # TODO: replace with real upstream API
        resp = await client.post("https://api.example.com/v1/run", json={{"input": req.input}})
        if resp.status_code != 200:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=resp.status_code, detail=resp.text)
        return resp.json()

# Run: uvicorn main:app --reload
